prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `57edb29d`.
codex_response:
Rule: the input is split by color `4` into rectangular panels; each decorated panel contributes marker cells by its `(distance to nearest top/bottom edge, distance to nearest left/right edge)` pairs, and the output is the unique undecorated panel with the union of those pairs painted into it. I used the summary hint as a starting hypothesis and kept it, since it matched the official examples; no `notes.md` was needed.

Implemented the task package in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_57edb29d/__init__.py), [helpers.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_57edb29d/helpers.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_57edb29d/verifier.py), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_57edb29d/generator.py). No new DSL primitive was needed.

Validation passed end-to-end: official verification passed `4/4`, the shared build produced `1000` examples in [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/57edb29d/task.json), and generated-example verification passed `1000/1000`. Preview artifacts were created at [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/57edb29d/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/57edb29d/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/57edb29d/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/57edb29d/previews/preview_03.png). I also visually checked `preview_01.png`; the generated family looks on-distribution, with one plain target panel and 2-3 decorated panels separated by color `4` backgrounds.
